call_transcript:
Agent: Hi Eleanor, this is Mark calling from Financial Solutions. I see you recently expressed interest in our Signature Credit Card. Um, how are you doing today? 
Eleanor: Oh, hi. I'm good, thanks. 
Agent: Great! So, based on your application, we're pre-approving you for a $5,000 Signature Credit Card at a rate of 14.99%. It comes with rewards points, travel insurance, and… uh… a low annual fee. 
Eleanor: Rewards points, huh? What kind of rewards? 
Agent: Well, you get 1.5 points for every dollar spent, and you can redeem them for cash back, travel, or merchandise. We also have a 0% introductory APR for the first six months. 
Eleanor: Six months… okay. And the annual fee? 
Agent: It's $99 a year. But honestly, with the rewards you'll earn, it more than pays for itself. 
Eleanor: Hmm. I'm a little worried about getting another credit card, I already have a couple. 
Agent: I understand. But this card is different. It's designed to help you build your credit and earn rewards. Plus, the 0% APR can save you money on interest. 
Eleanor: Okay, you know what? I think I'll go ahead and do it. 
Agent: Fantastic! Let me just confirm your details…

Agent: Hello, Mr. Miller? This is Sarah calling from Financial Solutions. We're offering a pre-approved Personal Loan. 
Robert: Where did you get my number? I didn't ask for this. 
Agent: Sir, we… uh… we acquired your information through a marketing partner. We're pre-approving you for a $10,000 Personal Loan at 18.5%. 
Robert: 18.5?! That's highway robbery! I already have a loan, and it's not that high. 
Agent: Well, sir, your credit score is a little lower… 
Robert: (Interrupting) My credit score is fine! You're just trying to rip me off. I don't need a loan from you. 
Agent: Sir, please… 
Robert: No, thank you. And take my number off your list! *click*

Agent: Hi Jessica, this is David from Financial Solutions. I'm calling regarding the Signature Credit Card. I understand you were referred by a valued partner. 
Jessica: Oh, yeah, my friend Sarah mentioned it. She said it was pretty good. 
Agent: Excellent! So, we're pre-approving you for an $8,000 Signature Credit Card with a 12.99% interest rate. It has fantastic travel rewards and a low APR. 
Jessica: Travel rewards are good. I'm planning a trip to Europe next year. 
Agent: Perfect! This card is ideal for that. You'll earn points on every purchase, and you can redeem them for flights, hotels, and car rentals. 
Jessica: What's the annual fee? 
Agent: It's $75. But the benefits far outweigh the cost, especially if you travel frequently. 
Jessica: Hmm. I'm a little hesitant about opening another card. I'm trying to keep my credit utilization low. 
Agent: I understand. We can start with a lower credit limit if you prefer. Maybe $4,000 to begin with? 
Jessica: Maybe… but I think I'd rather have the $8,000. Can we talk about the APR a little? Is that the best you can do? 
Agent: Let me see what I can do… Okay, I can offer you 12.5% if you agree to enroll in automatic payments. 
Jessica: Okay, 12.5% and automatic payments. Sounds good. Let's do it.

Agent: Hello, Michael? This is Lisa from Financial Solutions. I'm calling about the Personal Loan you inquired about online. 
Michael: (Sighs) Look, you guys called me yesterday about the same thing. I said I needed to think about it. 
Agent: I apologize for the inconvenience, sir. I just wanted to follow up and see if you had any questions. We are pre-approving you for a $15,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 16.99%. 
Michael: 16.99%? That's… pretty high. I was hoping for something lower. 
Agent: Well, your credit score is… uh… it's good, but not excellent. 
Michael: I know my credit score. I checked it. 
Agent: Right, of course. But we can offer you a flexible repayment plan. 
Michael: I don't know… I'm just not sure this is the right time. I need to shop around a bit more. 
Agent: Okay, I understand. Would you like me to send you some information via email? 
Michael: Yeah, that would be fine. Just… please don't call me again tomorrow.

Agent: Hello, Ms. Rodriguez? This is Kevin calling from Financial Solutions. We're offering a pre-approved Personal Loan. 
Patricia: (Sharply) What? I didn't ask for a loan. Where did you even get my number? 
Agent: Ma'am, we're pre-approving you for a $3,000 Personal Loan at 22.99%… 
Patricia: (Interrupting) 22.99?! Are you kidding me? This is ridiculous! *click*
